constraints.description: Activities are expected to start at TRL 2 and achieve TRL 5 by the end of the project.
All proposals are expected to incorporate mechanisms for assessing and demonstrating progress, including qualitative and quantitative KPIs, benchmarking, and progress monitoring.
When possible, proposals should build on and reuse public results from relevant previous funded actions.
Communicable results should be shared with the European R&D community through the AI-on-demand platform.
Projects selected in this topic should link to the resources offered by the AI Factories, including the Data Labs.
All proposals are expected to allocate tasks for cohesion activities with the co-programmed European Partnership on AI, data, and robotics (ADRA).
All proposals are expected to allocate tasks for cohesion activities with CSA HORIZON-CL4-2025-03-HUMAN-18: GenAI4EU central Hub.
Maximum EU contribution per project is 19,000,000 EUR. The indicative total topic budget is 38,000,000 EUR for an indicative 2 projects.
Proposals should build on or seek collaboration with relevant projects and develop synergies with other relevant International, European, national, or regional initiatives.

To ensure portfolio coverage, grants will be awarded to applications not only in order of ranking, but also to at least one proposal focusing on each of three sectors: healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and in-vehicle autonomous driving, subject to proposals passing all evaluation thresholds.

Financial Support to Third Parties (FSTP) provisions apply: maximum 300,000 EUR per third party at grant stage 2; maximum 2,250,000 EUR per third party at grant stage 3; maximum 2,550,000 EUR per third party total.
Submission follows a single-stage deadline model with a submission deadline of 2026-04-15.
This topic implements the co-programmed European Partnership on AI, data, and robotics (ADRA); proposals must align with and contribute to the ADRA co-programming framework.
